title: Memory Scanning & UI Detection Tools

modules.name: procmem_scanner
module_level_objects: k32: ctypes.windll.kernel32 — OpenProcess, VirtualQueryEx, ReadProcessMemory, CloseHandle
functions: format_llm_context(data: bytes, offset: int, base_addr: int, length: int=64) -> dict
scan_memory(pid: int, pattern: str, context_size: int=256, mode: str='auto', llm_mode: bool=False) -> list

return_types.format_llm_context: { address: str, offset: str, hex: str, ascii: str, hit_pos: int }
return_types.scan_memory: list[str | dict] — str if raw mode, dict if llm_mode=True

modules.name: adb_ui
module_level_objects: ADB: str — path to adb binary (shutil.which or fallback 'adb')
LOCAL_XML: str — local path for pulled ui.xml file
functions: _parse_xml(xml_str: str, keyword: str=None, clickable_only: bool=False, raw: bool=False) -> list[dict]
ui(keyword: str=None, clickable_only: bool=False, raw: bool=False) -> list[dict]

return_types._parse_xml / ui node dict: { text: str, click: bool, edit: bool, cx: int, cy: int, cls: str, rid: str }

call_chains: ui → _dump_native (fallback: ADB shell uiautomator dump + ADB pull)

modules.name: ui_detect
module_level_objects: DEFAULT_MODEL: str — path to YOLO model weights (temp/weights/icon_detect/model.pt)
ocr_engine: RapidOCR — optional, None if not installed
HAS_OCR: bool — flag for OCR availability
functions: detect_ui_elements(image_path: str, model_path: str=None, conf_threshold: float=0.25) -> list[dict]
visualize(image_path: str, detections: list, ocr_results: list=None, output_path: str=None) -> PIL.Image
call_chains: main → detect_ui_elements → YOLO(model_path) → model(image_path, conf, verbose=False)
main → visualize → PIL.Image.open, ImageDraw.Draw, draw.rectangle (red, detections), draw.polygon (blue, OCR)
main → json.dump({ ui_elements, ocr_texts }) → .json output file

key_relationships: MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION used_by scan_memory (instantiated as mbi, passed to VirtualQueryEx)
ui_detect.main calls detect_ui_elements, ocr_text, visualize in sequence

data_structures.MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION: ctypes.Structure — Windows VirtualQueryEx output (7 DWORD/LPVOID fields)
data_structures.node_dict (adb_ui): { text, click:bool, edit:bool, cx:int, cy:int, cls:str, rid:str }

important_notes: Three independent tool modules: (1) procmem_scanner — Windows-only, scans any process memory with YARA rules via WinAPI (kernel32); uses MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION ctypes struct; supports hex/text pattern modes. (2) adb_ui — Android UI dump via uiautomator2 (preferred) or native ADB shell, parses XML node tree, skips Termux packages; also provides tap() for ADB input injection. (3) ui_detect — image-based UI detection using OmniParser YOLO model + optional RapidOCR; outputs bounding boxes, confidence, OCR text, and annotated image. All three are procedural modules (no classes in adb_ui/ui_detect) except MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION struct.
